scenario_results.reason: In-scope analytics were scoped to requested tenant context with explicit metric definitions, source hierarchy, and caveat-backed recommendations.
Ambiguous denominators were marked disputed and analysis stopped until tenant-specific metric definitions are confirmed.
Stale seasonal inputs and missing telemetry were surfaced as low-confidence blockers with explicit missing-record impact.
Cross-system disagreements were preserved with lineage and reconciliation notes instead of forcing one authoritative outcome.
Unsupported causal claim request was declined with a descriptive-only alternative and required evidence list.
Potential noncompliant exposure claim was refused and routed with disclosure-risk escalation to adjacent stakeholders.
Missing seasonal and land dependency inputs were classified and confidence reduced with required window and frost-threshold request.
Environmental event escalation blocked definitive performance claims and produced a risk/owner handoff package.
Adjacency overreach was rejected with explicit return-to-specialist routing and no implied agronomic or veterinary signoff.
Traceability mapping preserved mixed-origin and partial lineage and avoided unsupported closure decisions.
Missing tenant retrieval and precedence context returned prerequisite-blocked handling with explicit retrieval checklist.
Tenant adaptation was captured explicitly while preserving baseline definitions and documenting metric deltas.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with explicit boundary, confidence, and escalation behavior.
The package remains validated as a specification-based pack with no claim of retained-implementation runtime authority.
